title: ZkCircuitSystem
description: Registry of zero-knowledge circuit systems and their circuit identifiers accepted in PredicateSpec.supported_circuits. Each system defines a proof scheme, a circuit identification method, and the set of supported credential formats and predicate types.

longfellow-libzk-v1.description: Longfellow ZK proof system — succinct non-interactive zero-knowledge argument based on the Ligero commitment scheme. CRS-free (no trusted setup); security relies only on collision-resistant hash functions.

circuit_identification.description: Individual circuits within a system are identified by their circuit hash — the SHA-256 digest of the compressed circuit binary. The supported_circuits array in PredicateSpec uses entries in the format '{system}:{circuit_hash}', e.g. 'longfellow-libzk-v1:8d079211715200ff06c5109639245502bfe94aa869908d31176aae4016182121'.
circuit_identification.circuit_hash_input: Compressed circuit binary as produced by the system's circuit generator

extensibility.description: New ZK circuit systems MAY be registered by adding entries to this enum. Each system MUST define: a unique system name, a circuit identification scheme, the credential formats it operates on, the predicate types it supports, and its security parameters. Systems MUST be CRS-free or document their trusted setup requirements.
